title: GitMesh
subtitle: Policy-as-Code Engine for Open-Source AI Agent Orchestration
highlights: Official Linux Foundation Decentralized Trust (LFDT) lab project - 140★, 48 forks, 562 commits, Apache-2.0; co-maintainer
Multi-agent orchestration runtime + governed MCP server: Triage, PR Review, Docs, Security, Community, Onboarding, and Release agents handle maintainer work autonomously
Policy-as-Code via OPA - maintainers write YAML that compiles to Rego; no agent merges a PR, edits CI, or files a CVE without human approval
Led the Phase Two pivot (v0.4.0) to an LF-aligned pnpm monorepo; native GitHub/GitLab webhook sync, ACP (JSON-RPC 2.0) agent-to-agent coordination, immutable audit log
Adopt with one YAML file + one CI step; MCP-compatible with Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI

caseStudy.problem: Open-source maintainers drown in triage, review, docs, and security work - and AI coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI) make it harder to govern: every tool acts with its own permissions, no shared policy, no audit trail. Letting autonomous agents touch a repo unsupervised risks runaway automation - an agent merging a bad PR, editing CI, or publishing a security advisory without a human in the loop.
caseStudy.approach: GitMesh is a multi-agent orchestration runtime and governed MCP server for OSS projects. Pre-defined agent roles (Triage, PR Review, Docs, Security, Community, Onboarding, Release) run on heartbeat schedules with token budgets and permission scopes. A single maintainer-defined Policy-as-Code layer - YAML compiled to OPA/Rego - governs every connected tool through MCP, so no agent merges a PR, edits CI/CD, or files a CVE without human approval. Native GitHub/GitLab webhook sync pushes agent actions to the forge; an ACP (JSON-RPC 2.0) orchestrator coordinates agents without conflicts or double work; every action is logged immutably with actor, policy version, and allow/block outcome. Any project adopts it with one YAML file and one CI step.

techDecisions.why: Maintainers express governance in simple YAML; compiling to Rego gives a battle-tested, auditable policy engine instead of ad-hoc permission checks.

techDecisions.decision: Governed MCP server over per-tool integrations
techDecisions.why: One MCP endpoint governs every AI coding tool (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Codex, Gemini) under the same policy - connect once, governed everywhere.
Lets multiple agents work simultaneously without double work, conflicts, or runaway cost.
Restructured cli / server / lib / agents / governance into one workspace to meet Linux Foundation lab standards and lower the barrier for external contributors.
Zero-config local dev with no external database, while supporting external Postgres via DATABASE_URL for production.

title: PM-JAY Claim Adjudication
subtitle: Automated Evidence Verification for Ayushman Bharat
highlights: Top 3 Runner-Up, National Health Authority × IISc AB-PMJAY Hackathon 2026 — ₹3L prize
End-to-end pipeline: ingest mixed-quality PDFs → multimodal extraction → episode timeline → STG rule engine → explainable Pass / Conditional / Fail
Primary + secondary Gemma 3 12B calls per page (doc taxonomy + per-package clinical flags) with two-tier disk cache for zero-token reruns
Hybrid VLM + rules: intersection fusion raised clinical F1 from 0.7567 → 0.7909 (+3.4 pts) vs pure-VLM on strict-label evaluator
Handles blur, 4 Indian languages + English, handwriting, stamps/signatures, QR/barcodes, negation-aware keywords, and multi-page rank consistency

caseStudy.problem: AB-PMJAY hospitals submit claim packets as blurry scans, photos, and multilingual PDFs — Hindi, Bengali, Gujarati, Assamese mixed with English, handwriting, rubber stamps, and 20+ document types where pre- vs post-treatment reports look alike. Manual Standard Treatment Guideline (STG) review cannot scale; missed negations (e.g. "no pallor"), hallucinated dates, and weak visual proof directly affect reimbursement.
caseStudy.approach: Five-stage claim adjudication pipeline: (1) ingest and normalize pages via PyMuPDF rasterization at 1.5× → 1024px JPEG; (2) multimodal extraction with dual cached Gemma 3 12B calls — primary for doc_type, OCR snippet, entities, and visual flags; secondary for 3–4 per-package clinical binary flags; (3) reconstruct episode timeline from earliest event occurrences; (4) negation-aware keyword layer + intersection fusion (VLM proposes → rules verify → both must agree, with numeric vitals bypass); (5) STG rule engine aggregates per-page outputs into case-level Pass (0.95), Conditional (0.75), or Fail (0.90) with full provenance (page · doc_type · confidence · evidence_excerpt).

techDecisions.decision: Intersection fusion (keyword ∧ Gemma) over pure VLM
techDecisions.why: Pure VLM hallucinates dates and confuses similar doc types; pure keywords miss handwriting and multilingual context. Intersection commits only when both agree — +3.4 clinical F1 on the live strict-label evaluator.
Cache hits cost zero tokens and zero latency; reruns stay within the ~750k token budget across 4 packages, cases, and pages.

techDecisions.decision: Laplacian blur score (threshold 80) + pyzbar visual detect
techDecisions.why: Flags poor-quality pages without dropping them; stamps, signatures, and QR/barcodes feed mandatory visual proof checks.
Stops false positives from "no pallor" / "denies fever"; Severe Anemia requires Hb < 7.0 g/dL on any page, not diagnosis text alone.

techDecisions.decision: RANK_MAP + SG039C continuity + per-package schema strip
techDecisions.why: Multi-page PDFs share canonical file rank when titles appear only on page 1; output must match PACKAGE_SCHEMAS key order exactly for compliance grading.

title: ClauseMark
subtitle: Verified Regulatory Evidence Engine - UN Digital Trade (RDTII)
highlights: UN Global Hackathon (Using AI for Digital Trade Regulatory Analysis) - clause-level evidence engine for RDTII governance indicators
15-package hexagonal monorepo (uv workspace), frozen Pydantic contracts; all 12 RDTII pillars as YAML, depth on Pillars 6 & 7

LangGraph pipeline (Postgres checkpointer + HITL interrupts): Docling/PyMuPDF/VLM-OCR extract → BGE-M3 + Qdrant hybrid + RRF + rerank → constrained-decoding classify → 4-gate verify → 3-state coverage
Anti-hallucination: the model emits only span IDs; deterministic ID-replacement citations; Layer-2 score is a human-confirmed recommendation, not an automated verdict
rie-eval gold-set runner with RAGAS metrics + ablations; FastAPI API + Streamlit audit UI; ruff / pyright / pre-commit and enforced architecture tests

caseStudy.problem: UN digital-trade benchmarking (RDTII) requires mapping real statutes to policy indicators with defensible evidence. Legal RAG systems routinely hallucinate citations; isolated clause retrieval misses exceptions and definitions in other sections; a confident "0" without measured recall is epistemically unsafe for regulators.
caseStudy.approach: Regulatory Intelligence Engine (RIE): parent-document hybrid retrieval over a legal structure graph; constrained decoding for indicator IDs; four verification gates (deterministic span checks + NLI + second LLM + self-consistency). Layer 1 is verifiable extraction; Layer 2 is a recommended score band that always requires human confirmation. Pillars and indicators live in YAML-not Python-so all 12 RDTII pillars extend by configuration.

techDecisions.why: The model never authors citation strings-only span IDs resolved by deterministic code, blocking ghost references.
Extraction is automatable and gate-checked; scoring is legal judgment and stays a reviewer-confirmed recommendation.
Child-chunk search with parent+neighbourhood context recovers cross-section exceptions standard chunking severs.
Engine stays generic; legal expertise ships in validated YAML + gold sets-add a pillar without refactoring core code.
Durable state and interrupt-based human review for flagged claims and absence cases.

title: PARAM
subtitle: Blockchain-Based Academic Credential Verification for ABV-IIITM Gwalior
highlights: Portal for Academic Records and Management - tamper-proof semester transcripts, degrees, and certificates for ABV-IIITM Gwalior
Semester workflow CSV → DRAFT → REVIEWED → APPROVED → ISSUED (NFT); degree flow with credit/backlog validation and academic + admin gates
ERC-721 on Base Sepolia (84532): SemesterNFT, DegreeNFT, CertificateNFT, CollegeRegistry, StudentRecords - SHA-256 PDF anchoring + revocation
Five roles: Student, Faculty, Academic, Staff, Admin (Privy); public Verifier - share links (7/30/90 days), QR on PDFs, API verify by token/hash/tokenId
Isolated BullMQ worker (blockchain · csv · notifications) - API container HTTP-only; on-demand Puppeteer PDFs; curriculum versioning + corrections/revaluation
Live pilot across 6 departments and 6 degree programs on AWS EC2 (Caddy, Express 5, worker, Redis) + Supabase Postgres; 100+ automated tests in GitHub Actions CI (Vitest, Foundry, Playwright)

caseStudy.problem: Universities issue transcripts and degrees as paper or static PDFs that are slow to verify and easy to forge. Registrars need configurable approval chains, curriculum versioning, SGPA/CGPA rules, and immutable audit trails. Students should never need crypto wallets; employers need sub-second verification with on-chain proof and optional deep-verify by document hash or NFT token ID.
caseStudy.approach: PARAM is a monorepo with six deployment layers: browsers (admin, academic, faculty, student, public verifier); Vercel edge (Next.js 16 App Router, HMAC param_session middleware, /api/v1 rewrites to EC2); AWS EC2 docker-compose (Caddy TLS → Express 5 api + isolated param-worker for BullMQ/cron); Supabase Postgres 15 + Prisma (37 models); Base Sepolia via ethers.js v6; Privy JWT auth, 5-role RBAC, Razorpay verify-flow, SMTP, Sentry. Feature modules cover students, curriculum, results, approvals, degrees, credentials, issuance, verification, and audit. Live pilot across 6 departments and 6 degree programs — faculty grades → academic review → admin mint → student share → verifier confirms.

techDecisions.why: Puppeteer, CSV ingest, blockchain mint, and five cron sweeps must never starve the HTTP Prisma pool - README mandates worker isolation.
No PDF object storage or background PDF queue - transcripts generated at download with DRAFT/APPROVED watermarks.

techDecisions.decision: Privy embedded wallets provisioned at login
techDecisions.why: Zero student seed-phrase friction; institute-controlled minting wallet for issuance.
Results: DRAFT→REVIEWED→APPROVED→ISSUED; degrees: PENDING_ACADEMIC→PENDING_ADMIN→ISSUED only when credits ≥ required and no backlogs.
Time-limited ShareLink tokens, QR embedding, and JSON API for automated employer checks - Verifier role needs no account.

techDecisions.decision: Zod-validated Express modules + full audit export
techDecisions.why: Every action logs actor, entity, metadata, and IP for registrar compliance; soft deletes on critical records.

title: SHAKTI
subtitle: Vision-Language Model (SHAKTI-2B) for Military Intelligence
highlights: SIH 2024 National Winner - built in a 40-hour sprint for problem statement PS1604
Fine-tuned mPLUG-Owl3 (SHAKTI-2B) with MS-SWIFT: VQAv2 80.1, POPE 87.4, NextQA 73.6, NLVR2 85.6
OCR accuracy 95% (printed) / 65% (handwritten) on degraded military documents
Redis batching cut the inference queue 2.5x on 4GB VRAM
Cross-modal RAG over mixed text + imagery; runs fully offline for air-gapped deployment

caseStudy.problem: Defense intelligence relies on dense, multi-page mixed-format documents - scans, maps, captioned imagery - that traditional OCR + keyword search misses. Analysts under time pressure need a system that retrieves semantically across both text and imagery, not just exact-match keywords.
caseStudy.approach: SHAKTI-2B is a fine-tuned mPLUG-Owl3 vision-language model (trained with MS-SWIFT) for OCR on degraded scans and cross-modal retrieval over text + imagery. Redis batching cut the inference queue 2.5x on 4GB VRAM; the whole stack runs offline for air-gapped deployment. Built in a 40-hour sprint for SIH 2024 problem statement PS1604.

techDecisions.why: Military fonts and degraded scans dropped standard pipelines below usable threshold.
A single index serves both text and image queries - no separate image search to maintain.
Analyst queries are open-ended and document corpora rotate; retrieval beats memorization.
Easy swap of LLM provider as the program of record evolves.

title: InferenceGym
subtitle: OpenEnv RL Environment for LLM Inference Serving
highlights: Submitted to the Meta × PyTorch × Hugging Face OpenEnv Hackathon ($30K prize pool; top finalists interview at Meta and Hugging Face AI teams)
OpenEnv-compliant RL environment modelling LLM serving as a control problem - batching, KV-cache budget, speculative decoding, quantization, prefill/decode split, priority routing
Reference PPO agent reliably outperforms Orca / vLLM-style hand-coded heuristics across static, bursty, and adversarial-multitenant workloads
Three deterministic tasks with per-step shaped rewards over throughput, SLO compliance, memory pressure, and cost
Deployed as a Hugging Face Docker Space with the full OpenEnv API surface (/reset, /step, /grader, /baseline, /web)

caseStudy.problem: LLM serving systems make many interdependent decisions per second - batching, KV-cache budget, speculative decoding depth, quantization tier, prefill/decode disaggregation, priority routing - under non-stationary workloads. Hand-coded policies (Orca, vLLM-style heuristics) plateau because the trade-offs shift with every traffic pattern. The Meta × PyTorch × Hugging Face hackathon asked for a true RL environment that exposes this as a learnable control problem.
caseStudy.approach: An OpenEnv-compliant environment modelling the serving control loop. Action space is a typed ServeAction (batch_cap, kv_budget_fraction, speculation_depth, quantization_tier, prefill_decode_split, priority_routing). Observations surface queue depth, p50/p99 TTFT, inter-token latency, throughput, KV occupancy, GPU memory, SLO compliance, and cost-per-1k. Per-step shaped reward over throughput + SLO + memory + cost. Three deterministic tasks (static / bursty / adversarial-multitenant). Reference PPO trained on CPU on the simulator; an OpenAI baseline path is wired in for submission compliance.

techDecisions.why: Round-1 grading is automated against the OpenEnv spec - typed schemas plus reset / step / grader / baseline endpoints are non-optional for submission.
Keeps training, grading, and reproducibility cheap and ships the environment as a small Docker image to a Hugging Face Space.

techDecisions.decision: Per-step shaped reward (throughput + SLO + memory + cost)
techDecisions.why: Pure terminal rewards collapse on bursty/adversarial workloads; per-step shaping gives the policy gradient a stable signal.

techDecisions.decision: PPO as the reference RL agent
techDecisions.why: Sample-efficient on the small simulator and a known-good baseline - the goal was demonstrating RL beats heuristics, not chasing SOTA.

techDecisions.decision: OpenAI baseline path via in-process runtime
techDecisions.why: Submission requires the official OpenAI client; in-process avoids the server having to call itself over HTTP and stays robust on Docker Spaces.

title: QuantumScribe
subtitle: OpenEnv RL Environment for LLM Quantum Error Correction
highlights: OpenEnv-compliant RL environment that teaches Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct to decode rotated surface-code syndromes using Stim ground truth and PyMatching baselines
Two-stage training on a free Colab T4: SFT on 3k PyMatching-labeled syndromes, then 1,500 GRPO steps with five independent verifiable rewards (no learned reward model)
96.4% logical correction rate at L2_target (d=3, 3 rounds, p=1e-3) - up from 92% base Qwen with the same prompt template; 73.4% exact-match with PyMatching
Deployed Hugging Face Docker Space with full OpenEnv API (/reset, /step, /healthz) plus published LoRA weights on the Hub
Inspired by DeepMind AlphaQubit (Nature 2024); honest eval discloses 0% PyMatching beat-rate on the primary slice

caseStudy.problem: Quantum processors need decoders that map stabilizer syndromes to Pauli corrections without measuring data qubits directly. PyMatching is a strong classical baseline (sparse blossom on detector graphs). DeepMind's AlphaQubit showed a transformer can beat it on hard cases, but at large-scale TPU training cost. The META RL Phase 2 track asked for an OpenEnv environment where an off-the-shelf LLM learns decoding from verifiable physics rewards.
caseStudy.approach: Built QuantumScribe (Qubit-Medic): a FastAPI OpenEnv server over Stim's surface_code:rotated_memory_z circuits with SI1000 noise. The LLM emits a terminal Pauli frame (X_ERRORS / Z_ERRORS); five independent rewards score logical correction, final-round syndrome consistency, Jaccard overlap vs PyMatching, format compliance, and a pymatching_beat bonus only when the model is right and PyMatching is wrong. Curriculum runs L1_warmup → L2_target → L3_stretch. Training: LoRA SFT on PyMatching labels, then GRPO with diversity-focused rollouts (temperature 1.2) so reward variance does not collapse.

techDecisions.why: GRPO games single scalars; decomposed Stim/PyMatching checks block empty-collapse, mimicry, and format spam by construction.
SFT ceiling is PyMatching imitation; RL needs on-policy rollouts against real syndromes to sharpen format and logical correction.
Same submission pattern as InferenceGym - typed reset/step, deployable Docker Space, trainer swaps local vs remote client.
Aligns with AlphaQubit/Willow literature and gives unfakeable logical_correction ground truth.
Primary eval shows match-not-beat; portfolio claims stay defensible for reviewers.

title: AlphaQ
subtitle: GRPO-Trained RL Agent for Minesweeper
highlights: GRPO fine-tuned Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct to reason about probabilistic, partially-observable boards
Phase-stratified training across 6 difficulty levels on 4,000 samples
Custom reward = JSON output purity + gameplay correctness + strategic bonuses
Stack-based iterative engine supporting 50×50 grids on AMD Instinct MI300X (192GB HBM3, ROCm/BFloat16)
Recognised by AMD for sophisticated technical implementation

caseStudy.problem: LLMs struggle with tasks requiring iterative reasoning under partial information. Minesweeper is a clean testbed: probabilistic, partially observable, and demands consistent JSON output across many turns. Most off-the-shelf models either hallucinate structure or fail to maintain constraints across moves.
caseStudy.approach: GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) fine-tuning on Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct via Unsloth + LoRA, with a custom reward combining JSON-output purity, gameplay correctness, and strategic bonuses. Phase-stratified curriculum across 6 difficulty levels on 4,000 samples. Trained on AMD Instinct MI300X (192GB HBM3) under ROCm in BFloat16. A stack-based iterative engine handles boards up to 50×50 without re-prompting full state.

techDecisions.why: Sample-efficient on small datasets and no separate value-model overhead.
Starting easy prevents reward collapse and unstable rollouts on hard boards.
Single signal kept outputs both syntactically valid and strategically coherent.
Avoids re-prompting full state on every move; scales to 50×50 without context blow-up.
Fits the 14B model plus GRPO group rollouts in a single device.

title: GitGambit
subtitle: One Place for a Developer's Public GitHub Story
highlights: Unified GitHub profile viewer - contributions, PRs, inline diffs (diff2html), and review threads without tab-hopping; no account required
External-work-first analytics: splits internal vs cross-org PRs/issues, org watchlist from saved profiles, and repo-grouped project trees
GitGambit Score (0–100): five sub-scores - velocity, quality, collaboration, consistency, range - from merge rate, time-to-merge, streaks, and language reach
Rule-based developer archetypes (OSS Ambassador, Night-Shift Builder, Polyglot, Refactorer, etc.) - pure heuristics, no LLM
Side-by-side compare up to 3 developers; timeline, insights heatmaps, language breakdown, and "what's new since last visit" on watchlist
SQLite + Drizzle cache: 1h fresh / 24h stale profile bundles; permanent PR diff cache; GraphQL pagination up to ~900 PRs per profile

caseStudy.problem: Reviewing a developer's real impact on GitHub means juggling profile, PR list, diff, and comment tabs - and own-repo commits hide the signal in cross-org OSS work. Recruiters and maintainers need external contributions, review quality, and activity patterns in one view, without building a custom script or burning API quota on every refresh.
caseStudy.approach: GitGambit (package github_analyzer) is a Next.js 16 App Router app that ingests a username via GitHub GraphQL (profile, up to 18 pages of PRs, 9 of issues, 5 of contributed repos) plus REST for per-PR file diffs. Results normalize into a ProfileBundle, cache in SQLite with stale-while-revalidate semantics, and render across routes: overview KPIs and heatmaps, org/repo drill-down, in-app PR viewer with unified/split diff modes and session-persisted viewed files, insights tab with archetype + composite score, timeline, and /compare for up to three profiles. Local watchlist (saved_users) tracks last-visited timestamps to surface what's new.

techDecisions.why: GraphQL aggregates PRs, issues, reviews, and calendar in fewer round-trips; REST /pulls/files returns patch hunks diff2html needs.
Personal tool model - no Redis required; merged PR diffs are immutable and stored permanently keyed by repo#number.
PRs where owner ≠ username are the clearest OSS signal; overview dashboard leads with external merged count.

techDecisions.decision: Slim RSC payloads for repo sidebar
techDecisions.why: Full PRNode bodies/comments stripped to SlimPR in project trees - MB-scale profiles shrink to tens of KB.
Deterministic, explainable labels for hiring screens; thresholds documented and tunable in lib/archetype.ts and lib/gitgambit-score.ts.

techDecisions.decision: Inflight dedupe on cold profile fetch
techDecisions.why: Layout + page both request the same bundle; Map-based coalescing avoids duplicate GraphQL bursts on cache miss.

title: iONIA Platform
subtitle: AI-Powered Competitive Exam Preparation System
highlights: NTA-replica exam interface with adaptive testing - 1,360+ precision-targeted questions
OCR-powered handwritten-answer assessment with real-time evaluation
Three-tier admin (super-admin / admin / student) serving 50+ active students
Selected for CrftHQ Cohort 1 accelerator program

caseStudy.problem: Indian competitive exam prep (JEE, NEET, etc.) needs an NTA-replica testing experience plus assessment of handwritten subjective answers - not just MCQs. Existing platforms are MCQ-only or rely on manual grading that doesn't scale beyond a handful of students.
caseStudy.approach: Next.js + Express + MongoDB stack with a dedicated FastAPI service for OCR-based evaluation of scanned handwritten responses. Adaptive testing engine across a 1,360+ question bank. Three-tier admin (super-admin / admin / student) for test, question, and user management. Selected for CrftHQ Cohort 1.

techDecisions.why: Keeps ML dependencies off the Node runtime; OCR service scales independently of the web app.
Schema flexibility - questions are heterogeneous (numerical, MCQ, subjective with image).
Students see progress instead of hitting fixed-difficulty walls; better retention.
Students upload scans of their actual handwritten work - no forced switch to a tablet flow.

title: GeneTrust AI Studio
subtitle: AI-Powered CRISPR Intelligence Platform
highlights: DNABERT-based genetic sequence analysis served via FastAPI + PyTorch
Next.js + TypeScript front-end with real-time ranking + prediction
RAG over published literature for cited-source results
On-chain provenance via Base Chain - every analysis hash anchored
HackHazards '25 Winner - top of 800+ projects

caseStudy.problem: Researchers using CRISPR for gene editing need fast, structured analysis of genetic sequences and predicted edit outcomes. Existing tools either run heavy locally or require deep bioinformatics tooling. The gap: a web platform where biologists submit a sequence and get model-driven analysis with verifiable provenance.
caseStudy.approach: DNABERT (a BERT pre-trained on the human genome) for sequence understanding, served via a FastAPI inference layer behind a Next.js + TypeScript front-end. Real-time ranking of edit candidates. Each analysis hash anchored on Base Chain for research-grade provenance. A RAG layer cites the published literature behind each prediction.

techDecisions.why: Pre-trained genome embeddings give a credible starting point for downstream classification with limited labeled data.
Async by default; easy per-tenant scaling once research traffic grows.
Research integrity matters more than tokenization; L2 economics keep cost per analysis well under $0.01.
Biologists need the cited paper, not just a number - RAG keeps the model honest.

title: AISEHack Phase 2 - PM2.5 Forecasting
subtitle: Episode-Aware Country-Scale Air Pollution Forecasting
highlights: Built a country-scale PM2.5 forecasting system over India from WRF-Chem gridded data
Trained for Phase 2 constraints: only 10-hour historical inputs and 16-step future predictions
Optimized for both global accuracy and episode-aware performance during extreme pollution events
Reached 0.8384 leaderboard score with Team Vega and qualified for finals at IIIT Hyderabad
Baseline runs: https://wandb.ai/ronitraj/aisehack_final | Final benchmark: https://wandb.ai/ronitraj/aisehack?nw=nwuserronitraj

caseStudy.problem: Short-term PM2.5 forecasting over India is difficult because pollution dynamics are strongly nonlinear and performance on average conditions often breaks down during severe episodes. Phase 2 explicitly evaluates both global quality and episodic behavior while restricting inference to a 10-hour lookback window.
caseStudy.approach: Built an episode-aware spatiotemporal forecasting pipeline over a fixed 140x124 India grid, predicting 16 future steps from 10-hour history. Trained on representative seasonal months from 2016 and inferred on unseen 2017 months. Used a hybrid deep-learning setup inspired by operator-learning and recurrent spatiotemporal modeling, with validation geared toward both global and episode metrics.

techDecisions.why: Phase 2 scoring emphasizes both global SMAPE and behavior on episodic grid points, so training/validation targeted this trade-off directly.
The task is naturally a sequence-of-fields problem; learning spatial transport and temporal evolution jointly improves stability.
Baseline and final experiments were tracked as separate runs to compare improvements and avoid overfitting to isolated submissions.
Competition requires a single executable submission notebook producing `/kaggle/working/preds.npy` with exact shape constraints.

title: ZeroKeyCLI
subtitle: Pay With Attention, Not Per Prompt
highlights: Watch a 5s ASCII ad, earn $0.001 USDC, get a free Claude API response
Two x402 micropayments settle on Monad in under 5 seconds - no wallet, no gas, no subscription
AdPool.sol smart contract manages advertiser deposits + verified impressions
Built with Node.js + ink.js terminal UI, settled on Monad via the x402 HTTP-native payment standard

caseStudy.problem: Developer-tool monetization is broken: subscriptions exclude casual users, terminals can't show ads, and crypto payments are too high-friction (wallet, gas, sign). Pay-per-use AI inference needs sub-cent settlement that disappears into a normal CLI flow.
caseStudy.approach: x402 micropayments on Monad. The user runs the CLI, sees a 5-second ASCII ad, and during those five seconds two micropayments settle on-chain - advertiser pays the user $0.001 USDC, user pays the AI provider for one Claude API response. AdPool.sol manages advertiser deposits and verified impressions. No wallet setup, no gas, no subscription.

techDecisions.why: HTTP-native payment standard composes cleanly with existing CLI tooling and HTTP middleware.
Sub-cent settlement is only economical on a high-throughput L1.
Renders the ad inside the CLI itself - no browser handoff breaks the developer flow.
Advertisers don't pay for fake renders; users aren't blocked by adversarial advertisers.

title: TypeNad
subtitle: Competitive On-Chain Typing Arena
highlights: PvP typing game on Monad blockchain with staking mechanics
Real-time 1v1 duels - speed and accuracy determine on-chain winners
Next.js + Monad architecture for high-throughput transactions
Deployed on NodeOpsHQ

title: HealthBooker
subtitle: Blockchain-Powered Medical Records Management Platform
highlights: AES-256-CBC encrypted medical records with IPFS decentralized storage
Ethereum smart contracts for patient-controlled granular access permissions
SHA-256 blockchain integrity verification with tamper detection
RainbowKit wallet integration for cryptographic record encryption + access control
Full transaction history with on-chain proof of all access and modifications

title: Aadhaar-Zero
subtitle: Privacy-Preserving KYC with Zero-Knowledge Proofs
highlights: Shortlisted among top teams nationwide for Stage III (CSIC 1.0, DSCI) - Privacy-Preserving KYC under Cryptography
End-to-end digital identity wallet: Aadhaar, PAN, DL, passport credentials with selective attribute disclosure
Groth16 age verification in-browser (Circom AgeVerifier + snarkjs) with on-chain nullifier anti-replay
Six smart contracts deployed and verified on Base Sepolia - consent, revocation, issuer/verifier registries, audit trail
4-step enrollment: AI document forensics, multi-modal liveness, BBS+-style proofs, QR verifier portal
DPDP Act compliance dashboard - on-chain consent, data minimization, erasure API, Merkle-anchored audit logs

caseStudy.problem: KYC forces users to overshare PII-banks and apps see full ID scans while fraudsters replay stolen documents. India’s DPDP Act demands consent trails, minimization, erasure, and unlinkable verification, but most stacks still centralize raw Aadhaar/PAN images.
caseStudy.approach: Aadhaar-Zero issues wallet credentials after AI document + liveness checks, then lets holders generate selective-disclosure proofs (attribute pick-list + QR). Groth16 proves age thresholds without revealing DOB; Poseidon nullifiers and on-chain registries block replay and record consent/revocation. PostgreSQL stores session state; Base Sepolia anchors trust; verifier API runs cryptographic + chain checks before approval.

techDecisions.decision: Dual ZK: Groth16 age + selective disclosure proofs
techDecisions.why: Age gates need arithmetic constraints in-circuit; attribute sharing needs a separate proof format for everyday KYC.
Separates issuer trust, verifier trust, nullifiers, consent, and revocation so each policy rule maps to an auditable contract.
One deployable demo app with typed DB models for credentials, verifications, risk, and audit batches.
Tamper-evident event log without putting full PII on-chain-only roots and hashes.
Document forensics and liveness reduce fake enrollments before any credential is signed or anchored.

title: E-Cell Management Platform
subtitle: Entrepreneurship Cell Website & Admin System
highlights: Complete entrepreneurship cell ecosystem with public site and administrative CMS
Three-tier access (Students, Admins, Super Admins) with granular permissions
Event registration and real-time updates via Redux Toolkit
Team showcase, resource library, and gallery with Cloudinary integration
Production-ready with TypeScript, JWT auth, and MongoDB Atlas

title: GraphRec Social Recommender
subtitle: Attention-Based GNN for Social Recommendation (from scratch)
highlights: From-scratch PyTorch implementation of GraphRec (Fan et al., 2019) - no graph library, hand-built attention aggregators
Models two graphs jointly: the user-item interaction graph and the user-user social trust network
Item-space + social-space user aggregation with item-level and social-level attention; item model aggregates rating users
Rating-aware opinion embeddings (user, item, and rating) fused through an MLP for rating prediction
Trained on the Epinions dataset (ratings + trust network); evaluated with MAE and RMSE on an 80/10/10 split

caseStudy.problem: Classic collaborative filtering ignores who users trust. On a platform like Epinions, a rating is shaped both by the items a user has rated and by the opinions of people in their social network. Capturing both signals - interactions and social trust - in one model is the core challenge of social recommendation.
caseStudy.approach: A from-scratch PyTorch implementation of GraphRec (Fan et al., 2019). Two coordinated GNN modules: a User model that aggregates the user's rated items (item-space) and their trusted friends' opinions (social-space), each with attention so more relevant items/friends weigh more; and an Item model that aggregates the users who rated an item. Rating-aware opinion embeddings combine item identity with the score given. User and item latent factors are concatenated and passed through an MLP to predict the rating. No graph library - the message passing, masking, and attention are built by hand in nn.Module.

techDecisions.decision: Implement GraphRec from scratch (no PyG / DGL)
techDecisions.why: Building the attention aggregators by hand forced a real understanding of message passing, masking, and opinion embeddings instead of treating the GNN as a black box.
A user's preferences come from both what they've rated and whom they trust; modeling only one loses the signal social recommendation depends on.
Not every rated item or trusted friend is equally informative for a prediction; learned attention weights the relevant ones.
Encoding the score alongside item identity lets the model distinguish a 5-star from a 1-star interaction on the same item.

title: LegalEase
subtitle: AI-Powered Legal Compliance & Tax Automation for Indian SMEs
highlights: 2nd overall + 3 category awards (Best Innovative Use Case, Best Technical Implementation, Best Presentation) at the Trillion Dollar GenAI Hackathon, IIT Bhubaneswar
6 specialized AI agents - Tax Filing Copilot, Compliance Health, Notice Responder, Document Generator, Trademark Assistant, and General Assistant
Automated ITR filing, GST returns, and compliance tracking with deadline alerts and a real-time compliance score
Blockchain document notarization - SHA-256 hashes anchored on Base via LegalEaseDocRegistry.sol for immutable verification
Next.js + FastAPI microservices with WebSocket live automation, OCR document management, and MongoDB Atlas

caseStudy.problem: Indian startups and SMEs juggle contracts, ITR/GST filing, legal notices, and compliance deadlines across disconnected tools and manual paperwork - slow, error-prone, and easy to miss a filing that triggers penalties. They need legal and accounting automation trustworthy enough to act on real documents, with proof the documents weren't tampered with.
caseStudy.approach: LegalEase is a microservices platform: a Next.js frontend, a FastAPI backend, MongoDB Atlas, and a Solidity contract on Base. Six specialized AI agents (Tax Filing Copilot, Compliance Health, Notice Responder, Document Generator, Trademark Assistant, General Assistant) automate filings, drafting, and monitoring instead of one general prompt. Documents are OCR'd, smart-categorized, and notarized on-chain - SHA-256 hashes stored via LegalEaseDocRegistry.sol on Base Sepolia for immutable verification. WebSockets stream live automation status, a GST Verification API validates returns, and a step-by-step onboarding flow sets up the entity, registrations, and required documents.

techDecisions.why: Store SHA-256 hashes via LegalEaseDocRegistry.sol for immutable proof-of-existence - without putting sensitive legal documents on a public chain.

techDecisions.decision: Six role-scoped agents over one general LLM
techDecisions.why: Tax, compliance, notices, and drafting need different tools and guardrails; scoped agents are more accurate and auditable than a single prompt.
Filing and compliance runs are long-running; streaming status keeps users informed instead of blocking on a request.

techDecisions.decision: Microservices split (Next.js / FastAPI / Solidity / GST API)
techDecisions.why: Keeps the Python automation services, the web app, and the on-chain layer independently deployable and scalable.

title: ReScroll
subtitle: TikTok for Research Papers

Gemini Pro generates concise 50-line summaries from complex research
Interactive D3-Force Knowledge Graph visualizing topic connections
Built with React Native + Expo for iOS, Android, and Web
Redis-powered caching with PostgreSQL + MongoDB hybrid storage

title: Mindsera
subtitle: AI-Powered Knowledge Tracking & Learning Ecosystem
highlights: Chrome extension tracking 30s+ browsing sessions to build personalized Knowledge Graphs
Groq LLM + Sentence-Transformers for semantic embeddings on browsed content
Neo4j Knowledge Graph maps concept relationships; Qdrant for vector search
Dual-backend architecture (Node.js + FastAPI) processing real-time learning analytics
Social-matching algorithm finds study partners by Knowledge Graph overlap

title: First Website
subtitle: Where It All Started - Static HTML/CSS, 2019
highlights: My first public repo (July 2019): a Radisson Blu–inspired hotel landing page - two HTML pages, no framework
Responsive navbar with mobile sidenav, destination cards, and read-more toggles in vanilla JavaScript
Learned layout with flexbox, media queries at 900px, and Git workflow (feature branch + merged PRs)
The arc from this static clone to production apps (PARAM, GitGambit, portfolio) is the real story
